meta_title: For Founders: email automation to Solve tutorial hell (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome tutorial hell. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Email Automation to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Break free from tutorial hell. Use this advanced checklist to build high-converting email automation using Antigravity and Claude Code for your SaaS.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop consuming and start shipping. By leveraging Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you can automate your SaaS onboarding sequence in hours. Focus on high-intent triggers, personalized delivery, and measurable conversion metrics to turn passive users into paying customers.
content.intro:
You are drowning in a sea of 'Hello World' tutorials while your competitors are shipping features that actually capture revenue. The dopamine hit of watching another video is a trap; it is a phantom of productivity designed to keep you stagnant. You are not a student; you are a Founder.

It is time to pivot from consumption to execution. We are stripping away the complexity of email automation by pairing the structural power of Antigravity with the raw coding velocity of Claude Code. This isn't just about sending emails; it's about building an automated revenue engine that works while you sleep.

steps.title: Define the High-Intent Trigger
steps.description: Identify the exact moment in your user journey where email intervention drives conversion, such as a 'Sign-up' without an 'API Key generation'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Architect a database schema and event-listener logic for a SaaS onboarding flow. Define triggers for 'User Signed Up' and 'Action Not Completed' events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create event listener for user_signup event in /services/events.ts'

steps.title: Abstract Email Service Provider Logic
steps.description: Decouple your application logic from the email provider (e.g., Postmark or Resend) to ensure vendor agnosticism.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular email service provider interface with methods for transactional and sequence emails.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement email-service.ts interface with provider-agnostic methods'

steps.title: Build the Sequence Engine
steps.description: Implement a state-machine that tracks user progress through your drip campaign without creating spaghetti code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a state-machine implementation for a 3-part email sequence based on user activity timestamps.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create sequence-manager.ts to handle scheduling and state tracking'

steps.title: Dynamic Template Orchestration
steps.description: Use template variables to inject user-specific data, drastically increasing open and click-through rates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a template engine integration that maps database user objects to email body placeholders.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Integrate template-engine.ts to inject user name and action links'

steps.title: Implement Webhook Feedback Loops
steps.description: Capture bounce, open, and click events to automatically segment users and optimize your funnel performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a webhook controller to ingest delivery status events and update the user's engagement score in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Implement webhook-handler.ts for SES/Resend delivery events'

pro_tips: Never hardcode email copy; treat content as external configuration files to allow for rapid A/B testing without redeploying code.
Implement a 'cooldown' period in your sequence engine to prevent spamming users who perform multiple actions in one session.
Always include a 'magic link' in your emails that bypasses password entry to reduce friction in your conversion loop.
Track 'Time-to-First-Value' as your primary KPI; if your email doesn't move them toward that value, delete the email.
Use a local mock-server during development to simulate high-volume email traffic without hitting your production API limits.
content.next_steps: Once your sequence is live, perform a 'smoke test' with a real account, monitor the logs for delivery failures, and then begin A/B testing subject lines to optimize for a 40%+ open rate.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for the sake of building. Start building to scale. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
